Iranians celebrating peace and supporters of Field Marshal Asim Munir

Once reports emerged that an Iran-U.S. peace deal was becoming a reality, some hardline religious groups and Pahlavi supporters in Iran took to the streets to protest the agreement. The protests received little attention globally.

Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced news of peace, which was  endorsed by both the U.S. and Iran. It claims that a formal signing ceremony is expected this Friday in Geneva, hosted by Pakistan.

After the announcement, most Iranians expressed gratitude and celebrated. In Iran right now, when people see Pakistanis, they greet them enthusiastically with “Thank you, Pakistan” and “Thank you, Syed Asim Munir” instead of the usual greeting.

Turkish businesswoman Canan Çelebioğlu:  
We entered India and became the largest ground handling company there.   
I was obsessed with India 
I called it my second country.  
Last year, around May 15th, the Indian government shut us down.   
They seized all our equipment, transferred 10,000 employees to another company in a single day — and wiped out a value we had built of perhaps $400–500 million.   
Gone in one day.  
Put the financial loss aside. 
That place was built stitch by stitch.   
We spent years pushing the government to change policies, to develop the sector.   
And I loved India. 
That's what made it so shocking. It truly devastated us

"د ایمان خوږوالی" 

که مینه وي او که کرکه، که ملاتړ وي او که مخالفت —
که دا ټول د عدل او حقیقتونو پابند او د الهي شریعت تابع نه وي، نو تعصب زېږوي.

او تعصب هغه تر ټولو ناوړه ناروغي، بلکې د ټولو ناروغیو مور ده، چې د ګمراهۍ لامل، د ظلم ریښه او د فساد بنسټ ګرځي.

د دې آخرت تباه کوونکې ناروغۍ درملنه "الحب في الله والبغض في الله" ده — يعني د الله لپاره مینه او د الله لپاره کرکه.

په حدیث مبارکه کې دا د ایمان تر ټولو ټینګه کړۍ بلل شوې ده.

که دا کړۍ ټینګه وي، نو د ایمان خوږوالی به درته نصیب شي، پر دین به ثبات در په برخه شي او الله سبحانه وتعالی به د فتنو او فسادونو په تیارو کې د خیر او هدایت د مشعل ګرځېدو توفیق درکړي.

الله تعالی دې موږ ته توفیق راکړي چې زموږ مینه او کرکه د ځان، تنظیم، ډلې، قوم او وطن لپاره نه وي، بلکې خالص د الله تعالی د دین لپاره وي.

Tribal Ultimatum Expires, Terrorist Mohammad Fateh Alias Kaka Wazir Killed Along with Son and Nephew

Outlawed TTP militant commander Mohammad Fateh alias Kaka Wazir was killed in a clash with Bannu police and peace committee officials, along with his son and nephew.

According to local sources, the peace committee had given him a three-day ultimatum on Friday to leave Mir Ali. Sources say he did not comply with the tribal decision and arrived at the police station with the intent to attack, where he was killed along with his son and nephew in an exchange of fire.

After several days of presenting themselves as victims and criticizing state institutions, supporters of the government claim that the group's true nature has now been exposed.

A video recovered from the mobile phone of an arrested participant appears to show an individual associated with the movement aiming an SMG at law enforcement personnel.

This incident reveals the reality behind what they describe as a so-called struggle for public rights. The footage should challenge the belief that the protests are entirely peaceful and support the view that the group has engaged in disorderly or violent activities.

The movement promotes an agenda of hatred, violence, and unrest, and argues that strict action against it is necessary. This is not an isolated incident, but part of a pattern of violent behaviour that has occurred since the beginning. Such incidents justify the government's decision to declare the group illegal.

These scenes from Rawalakot in Pakistan's Azad Jammu and Kashmir show individuals associated with the banned Joint Action Committee using automatic weapons during clashes with law enforcement personnel.

Under Pakistani law, Article 5 of the Constitution and Sections 121 and 122 of the Pakistan Penal Code address waging war against the state and the collection of men, arms, or ammunition with the intent to wage war against the state. Such acts are considered high treason and are punishable by death
